content.problemStatement:
There are NNN friends at a party playing a game called **Balloon Smash**. Each friend iii has an annoyance factor AiA_iAi​.

If the iii-th friend is hit by AiA_iAi​ balloons, they immediately leave the party.

The game proceeds according to the following rules:


The friends take turns sequentially from 111 to NNN.
If the current friend has already left the party, the turn moves to the next friend.
If there is no next friend available, the game ends.
When it is friend iii's turn and they are still present in the party:

Friend iii hits **every other friend currently present in the party** with **exactly one balloon**.
After doing so, friend iii leaves the party.
Then, for any remaining friend jjj who has been hit by AjA_jAj​ balloons so far, this friend will **immediately leave the party**.


Your task is to determine how many balloons hit each friend by the time the party ends.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines:

The first line contains a single integer NNN, the number of friends.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, where AiA_iAi​ is the annoyance factor of the iii-th friend.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing NNN integers.

The iii-th integer should denote the **total number of balloons that hit friend iii** by the end of the party.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2×1052 \times 10^52×105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: N=3,A=[2,2,2]N = 3, A = [2, 2, 2]N=3,A=[2,2,2].
Initially, all friends {1,2,3}\{1,2,3\}{1,2,3} are in the party and everyone has been hit by 000 balloons.
Turn 1 (Friend 1 is present):

Friend 1 hits every other present friend: 2 and 3 get +1 each.
Balloons received = [0,1,1][0, 1, 1][0,1,1]
No one has reached their limit yet (limit is 222 for all).
Friend 111 leaves the party.
Present friends = {2,3}\{2,3\}{2,3}

Turn 2 (Friend 2 is present):

Friend 2 hits every other present friend: 3 gets +1.
Balloons received = [0,1,2][0, 1, 2][0,1,2]
Friend 2 leaves after finishing their turn.
Friend 3 has now been hit by 2 balloons, which equals A3=2A_3 = 2A3​=2, so Friend 3 leaves immediately.
Present friends = {}\{\}{}

No friends remain, so the game ends.
Final balloons received by each friend = [0,1,2][0, 1, 2][0,1,2].
Test case 222: The game proceeds as follows:

Turn 111: Friend 111 hits everyone else with a balloon and leaves.
Because A2=1A_2 = 1A2​=1, friend 222 also leaves.
Turn 222: Friend 222 is not present any longer, so we skip to friend 333, who hits the only remaining person (friend 444) with a balloon, and then leaves.
Turn 333: Friend 444 still remains, but there's nobody else to hit with a balloon.
The game ends.

The final counts are [0,1,1,2][0, 1, 1, 2][0,1,1,2].
